LESSON 1 — Exploring the Generative AI Hub
Objective: Identify the AI Foundation and the Generative AI Hub.
What is the AI Foundation?
Core Definition
The AI Foundation is SAP's AI operating system — the backbone of SAP Business AI. It enables organizations to
build, extend, and run custom AI solutions at scale in a secure, responsible, and efficient manner on SAP
Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP).
Why AI Foundation Matters (Key Problems It Solves)
 High resource demands for onboarding AI models
 Fragmented tools — no central governance or collaboration hub
 Difficulty scaling AI responsibly across regions and regulations
AI Foundation addresses these with:
 Unified Development & Orchestration Platform — central, integrated access for all AI Foundation tools
 Built-in Governance & Security — ethical, secure, compliant AI from the ground up
 Seamless Integration — connects SAP and non-SAP data, infrastructure, and partner models
Core Products of AI Foundation
Product
Purpose
Joule Studio
Low-code/no-code environment for building AI agents and extending Joule
skills
Generative AI Hub
Secure managed access to top-tier LLMs + advanced prompt engineering
tools — core focus of Module 3
SAP Document AI
Automates data handling from unstructured documents; enhances data
quality in business workflows
SAP Knowledge Graph
Connects SAP and external data — creates contextual intelligence for
smarter AI outcomes
SAP Foundation Model
Produces reliable predictions from SAP application data; addresses LLM
limitations
AI Foundation Architecture — 4 Layers
Layer
What It Does
Key Role
1. OS Interfaces Layer
Unified entry point for dev/ops/admin.
Houses AI Playground, Joule Studio.
User-facing tools; intuitive interaction
2. AI Kernel Layer
Core — manages AI agents, workloads,
model lifecycle, resource
allocation/scheduling.
Secure, compliant, efficient AI operations
3. AI Integration Layer
Connectivity to data sources, models,
services. Supports workload
management + AI model orchestration.
Data integration pipelines across
environments

4. Peripheral & Data Layer
Integrates SAP + non-SAP data, existing
IT infrastructure, partner models.
Raw materials + compute power for AI
processes
SAP AI Core
SAP AI Core is the BTP component that enables management, execution, and operation of AI assets in a
standardized, scalable, hyperscaler-agnostic way.
Uses Resource Groups — virtual folders to keep ML resources and tasks separate. Each account gets a default
resource group; admins can add/remove via SAP AI Launchpad.
The Generative AI Hub — Core Role
The Generative AI Hub lives inside SAP AI Core and provides systematic, tool-assisted model selection, access, and
exploration of LLMs grounded in business and contextual data.
How it delivers SAP's 3 Promises (Relevant · Reliable · Responsible):
Principle
What It Means
How GenAI Hub Delivers It
Relevant
LLM responses grounded in YOUR
enterprise data — not generic outputs
Grounding via SAP HANA Vector Engine;
finds relevant business docs for LLM
context
Reliable
Trusted, continuous access to multiple
LLMs; no single-vendor lock-in
Supports diverse providers; auto/manual
model version upgrades for business
continuity
Responsible
Security, compliance, data protection by
design
Content filtering, data masking,
anti-injection, SOC 2 / NIST / ISO
certified. SAP NEVER shares customer
data with external vendors for model
training.
Key Capabilities of the Generative AI Hub
Capability
Detail
Access to Multiple Models
Streamlined access to frontier AI models from various providers; flexible
compute allocation
Model-Agnostic Development
Switch between models freely; adapt orchestration configs on the fly; manage
prompt variants per LLM
Exploration & Development
Prompt editor, prompt registry/library, SDKs, AI Playground for secure model
experimentation
SDKs & Client Libraries
SAP Cloud SDK for AI — official SDK for AI Core + GenAI Hub.

Integrates
with SpringAI, LangChain, LangGraph
Orchestration
Coordinates multiple LLMs, manages data flow, integrates content
moderation + data masking, end-to-end AI workflow management
Deployment & Delivery
Bring Your Own Model (fine-tuned open-source / proprietary) — deploy and
manage full lifecycle
Governance
Comprehensive logging, auditing, metering, monitoring, multi-tenancy, role
management — all compliant with regulatory standards

Model Lifecycle — 3 Hosting Models
Type
Description
Key Benefit
Self-Hosted Models
Org deploys its own (fine-tuned /
proprietary) models
Full control of infrastructure, scaling,
security, versioning, costs
SAP Managed Models
Pre-integrated managed access to
3rd-party LLMs (e.g., GPT-5, Gemini 2.5
Pro, Claude Sonnet 4). SAP handles
commercial agreements, data privacy,
security.
No separate vendor licenses needed;
SAP responsible for compliance
SAP Hosted Models
Models hosted on SAP's own
infrastructure; pre-configured for SAP
ecosystem
SAP manages all ops — security,
performance, compliance — fully
integrated
Model Version Management
LLMs evolve — providers release new versions and retire old ones.
GenAI Hub supports AUTO UPGRADE and MANUAL UPGRADE strategies. You can monitor deprecation dates
and ensure continuity without disrupting your applications.
How to Access the Generative AI Hub
 Set up SAP BTP global account → Provision SAP AI Core → Assign roles/permissions
 Access via SAP AI Launchpad (UI) or programmatically via APIs and SDKs
 Deploy LLMs from various providers, configure model type/version
 Each deployed LLM gets a secure endpoint for enterprise app integration
LESSON 2 — Generative AI Applications Beyond Chatbots
Objective: Explain GenAI applications using software-driven input/output
scenarios.
The 4 Interaction Types (Exam-Relevant Framework)
Interaction Type
Description & SAP Example
Human → AI
Human gives natural language input; AI produces text/content output.
Example: Business analyst asks AI to summarize support tickets by product
category.
Software → AI
Backend system feeds structured data; AI generates natural language report.
Example: SAP backend feeds sales order data → AI highlights fulfillment
bottlenecks.
Human → Software via AI
Human describes intent; AI produces executable code or triggers system
action. Example: Developer describes OData service → AI generates
boilerplate code structure.

Example: SAP backend feeds sales order data → AI highlights fulfillment
bottlenecks.
Human → Software via AI
Human describes intent; AI produces executable code or triggers system
action. Example: Developer describes OData service → AI generates
boilerplate code structure.
Software → Software via AI
Automated process triggers AI which initiates another system action — no
human in the loop. Example: SAP SuccessFactors detects understaffing → AI
initiates recruitment workflow in ATS.

Key Insight
Generative AI is NOT just chatbots. It can independently produce reports, execute software commands, translate
content, and drive automated workflows — entirely without constant human input. The boundaries between
interaction types overlap; prompt engineering integrates human + software processes.
Practical Use Case: Consistent Product Description Generation
 Problem: Manufacturing company struggles with inconsistent product descriptions across sales channels →
customer confusion, brand erosion
 Solution: Deploy LLM via GenAI Hub, feed core specs from SAP PLM → auto-generate consistent, engaging
descriptions
 Value: Brand consistency, improved customer understanding, boosted sales, freed marketing teams from manual
work
LESSON 3 — Discovering the Generative AI Hub Interface
Objective: Navigate the Generative AI Hub interface in SAP AI Launchpad.
SAP AI Launchpad — Overview
 Multi-tenant SaaS application on SAP BTP
 Used to manage AI use cases (scenarios) across multiple AI runtime instances (e.g., SAP AI Core)
 Primarily for administrators and developers — business users access GenAI Hub directly
Main Apps in SAP AI Launchpad
App
Purpose
Workspaces
Manage API connections and organize resources. Each API connection =
one tenant, containing multiple resource groups.
Generative AI Hub
Core app — model management, prompt development, LLM application
lifecycle oversight.
SAP AI Core Administrator
Admins manage Git repos, app deployments, Docker registry, connection
secrets — secures GenAI Hub access.
ML Operations
Manage scenarios, executables, configurations; deploy models +
orchestration services; manage datasets, models, artifacts.
Generative AI Hub Interface — Key Components
1. Model Library
 Shows all available models with metadata — helps select the right model for your use case
 Catalog Mode — browse with model cards (data input types, costs, performance metrics); initiate deployment
 Leaderboard Mode — compare models by benchmark scores; filter, sort, compare context window vs cost
 Chart Mode — visual comparison of any two benchmarks/parameters (e.g., context window vs output token cost)
Use Case Tip
Need to summarize long internal reports?

Use Model Library to compare context window size + cost per output
token to find the most efficient and affordable model.
2. Chat Interface
 Conversational platform for interacting with models; context maintained within session

 Select any deployed model via 'Configure chat settings' gear icon
 Update parameters, context history, message roles, and variables
 Download chat as JSON; copy individual messages (images excluded)
 Best for: rapid prototyping of conversational flows and testing LLM responses (Human-to-AI interaction)
3. Grounding Management
 Manages lifecycle of data pipelines that provide context to LLMs
 Connects documents stored in SharePoint or cloud storage (e.g., S3) as context sources for LLMs
4. Prompt Editor
Feature
Detail
Message Blocks
Dedicated area for System, Assistant, and User role prompts. Multiple blocks
per version.
Variable Definition
Add variables to make prompts flexible and reusable. Use defaults or runtime
values via SDK.
Model Selection
Select from available GenAI Hub models; modify parameters directly in editor.
Metadata
Add tags and notes to categorize and manage prompts effectively.
Save & Version Mgmt
Save prompts with collection names; save multiple versions for traceability
and iterative refinement.
Response
View, save, and continue editing response in same window; compare
versions.
Use: Primary interface for creating, managing, and retrieving prompts for prototyping and prompt lifecycle management.
Supports Human-to-AI and Software-to-AI interactions.
5. Prompt Management
 Central library of all saved prompts — view all versions and models used
 Select a version to open in Prompt Editor for reuse or modification
 Ensures consistency, saves development time, prevents duplication across teams
6. Orchestration Interface
 Designs and tests orchestration workflows — covered in detail in Lessons 4 & 5
 Enables dynamic prompt population with real-time data during inference
 Executes complex business logic across chained AI modules
LESSON 4 — Discovering the Orchestration Service
Objective: Identify the Orchestration Service — purpose, features, prerequisites.
What is the Orchestration Service?
Core Definition
A managed service within SAP AI Core that provides unified access, control, and execution of generative AI
models.

What is the Orchestration Service?
Core Definition
A managed service within SAP AI Core that provides unified access, control, and execution of generative AI
models. It acts as a central coordinator, managing multiple AI models and services to complete complex tasks via
a single API call.
Why Orchestration is Essential for Enterprise AI
Challenge Solved
How Orchestration Solves It

Seamless Integration & Provider
Agnosticism
Harmonized API — interact with different LLMs without coupling to specific
provider. Switch/compare models without changing core app logic.
Enhanced Control & Compliance
Built-in mechanisms for SAP standards, privacy regulations, ethical
guidelines. Data masking + anonymization ensure compliance across all AI
workflows.
Increased Efficiency & Scalability
Complex workflows chain outputs to inputs automatically. Supports
high-volume enterprise deployments.
Expandability & Adaptability
Easily add new features (content filtering, masking, grounding, translation) as
AI market evolves — future-proof solutions.
Key Features (Modules) of the Orchestration Service
Module
Purpose & Key Detail
Grounding
Integrates external/domain-specific/real-time data (SAP systems, Help Portal,
internal docs) to enhance contextual relevance and combat hallucinations.
Templating
Create prompts with placeholders; dynamically populated with real-time data
during inference. Makes prompts flexible and reusable.
Content Filtering
Restricts harmful/toxic/inappropriate content entering or leaving LLM.
Maintains compliance and safety. Configurable strictness (e.g., 'relaxed'
mode for business terms).
Data Masking
Anonymizes/pseudonymizes PII before it reaches the LLM. Can unmask in
responses when pseudonymization is used. Ensures data privacy while
maintaining utility.
Translation
Translates input/output data within the workflow — enables multilingual
enterprise use cases without changing application logic.
Prerequisites for Using the Orchestration Service
 Active SAP BTP Account
 SAP AI Core Instance set up within BTP
 Extended SAP AI Core Service Plan (GenAI Hub not available in free/standard tiers)
 At least one Orchestration Deployment within a resource group (default deployment provided; custom deployments
enable model customization)
SDK Usage
The SAP Cloud SDK for AI provides a simple way to use the Orchestration Service. Set up a template, connect to
an LLM (e.g., GPT-5 or Gemini 2.5 Pro), and automate tasks like translation or content generation with minimal
code.

Set up a template, connect to
an LLM (e.g., GPT-5 or Gemini 2.5 Pro), and automate tasks like translation or content generation with minimal
code. Once deployed, the ENTIRE multi-step workflow runs from a SINGLE API CALL.
LESSON 5 — Orchestration Use Cases & Workflow Design
Objective: Demonstrate the value of orchestration through practical use cases.
Standard Orchestration Workflow — 9 Steps (MEMORIZE THIS)

Key Rule
The ORDER of modules is FIXED (for security, data integrity, compliance). But the CONFIGURATION of each
module is flexible — you control whether it's active and how it behaves.
1
User Input
Initial query/data from end-user or upstream application
2
Grounding
Retrieves relevant facts from enterprise repos (SAP Help Portal, internal docs, master
data)
3
Templating
Combines grounded data + user input + system instructions into a comprehensive prompt
via pre-configured template
4
Input Masking
Scans prompt for PII (emails, names, addresses, phone numbers) → pseudonymizes
before LLM sees it
5
Input Filtering
Content safety scan (e.g., Azure Content Safety) for harmful/toxic content BEFORE
sending to LLM
6
Input Translation
Translates prompt into LLM's operating language (e.g., German → English) if needed
7
LLM Processing
Prepared, safe, masked, filtered, translated prompt sent to selected LLM → generates
response
8
Output Filtering
Safety scan of LLM response — removes toxic/harmful content BEFORE returning to user
9
Output Translation
Translates LLM response back to user's native language (e.g., English → German) if
needed
Use Case 1: Automated Technical Support Response (German Customer Ticket)
Business Problem
Customer submits German technical support ticket for SAP Signavio including their email address. System must:
provide accurate policy-aligned response, protect PII, handle language barrier, summarize for human agent if full
answer unavailable.

System must:
provide accurate policy-aligned response, protect PII, handle language barrier, summarize for human agent if full
answer unavailable.
 Input: German email with SAP Signavio question + personal email address
 Grounding: Keywords extracted → queries help.sap.com → retrieves relevant Signavio notification articles
 Templating: System prompt = 'helpful support assistant; check context, answer or summarize for human team'
 Input Masking: Email address pseudonymized to [profile-email]
 Input Filtering: Set to 'RELAXED' mode — allows broader range of input to prevent blocking legitimate business
terms
 Input Translation: German → English for LLM processing
 LLM: Processes enriched, safe, English prompt → generates answer or summary
 Output Filtering: Safety scan on LLM output
 Output Translation: English → German for customer
Business Value: PII protected · Grounded answers · Language barrier eliminated · Labor-intensive support
automated · HR focus on strategic work
Use Case 2: Internal HR Feedback Analysis (English Feedback Forms)
Key Learning from This Use Case
Modules can be SKIPPED when not needed. Since input is already English and output is needed in English, both
Translation modules are omitted — simplifying the workflow and REDUCING COST.

 Input: English employee feedback forms uploaded by HR analyst
 Grounding: Keywords (benefits, work-life balance) → retrieves relevant HR policies from knowledge base
 Templating: System prompt = 'HR analyst; summarize themes, sentiment, actionable insights; ensure anonymity;
flag policy violations'
 Input Masking: Pseudonymizes employee names, IDs, sensitive dept names from feedback
 Input Filtering: RELAXED — avoids blocking legitimate business terminology
 Input Translation: SKIPPED (already English)
 LLM: Analyzes feedback against grounded HR policies → generates summary + thematic analysis
 Output Filtering: Scans output for toxic language, bias, inappropriate suggestions
 Output Translation: SKIPPED (English output required)
Business Value: Anonymity ensured · Rapid insight from large feedback volumes · Grounded in actual HR policy
· Cost optimized (no unnecessary translation)
Design & Deployment Summary
 Workflows defined as JSON templates via SAP Cloud SDK for AI — specifies the sequence of services
 Fixed logical sequence + dynamic per-module configuration = balance of reliability and adaptability
 Single API call executes the ENTIRE multi-step pipeline — complexity hidden from the application layer
 Dramatically simplifies development, ensures consistency, enables rapid deployment and modification

KNOWLEDGE QUIZ — All Questions & Answers
Unit 1 Quiz — Get 3/4 correct to pass
Q: Which of the following components are available within the Generative AI Hub interface in SAP AI
Launchpad? (Select all that apply — 3 correct)
✔ Model Library for model selection and comparison
✔ Chat interface for interactive model testing
✔ Grounding Management for data pipeline lifecycle
✔ Prompt Editor and Prompt Management for prompt creation and reuse
Why: All four are correct: Model Library, Chat, Grounding Management, and Prompt Editor/Prompt Management are all
components of the GenAI Hub interface.
Q: Which type of interaction exemplifies 'Software-to-Software via AI'?
✔ An automated recruitment workflow initiated by AI in a connected applicant tracking system
✗ A developer describes a feature and AI produces the code structure
✗ A business analyst gets a textual summary of support tickets
✗ A backend system feeds sales order data to AI, generating a report
Why: Software-to-Software via AI = SAP SuccessFactors detects understaffing → AI initiates recruitment workflow in ATS.
No human is in the loop.
Q: What is the primary role of SAP's AI Foundation within SAP BTP?
✗ To provide analytical and visualization tools for non-business users
✔ To serve as SAP's AI operating system, enabling secure, scalable, and responsible AI development
✗ To serve as SAP's AI database, enabling secure, scalable, and responsible AI development
✗ To augment SAP HANA as the main database for AI workloads
Why: AI Foundation = SAP's AI operating system on BTP. It enables Build, Extend, and Run of custom AI solutions with
built-in relevance, reliability, and responsibility.
Unit 2 Quiz — Get 2/2 correct to pass
Q: What is the primary reason the Orchestration Service uses a fixed sequence of modules?

It enables Build, Extend, and Run of custom AI solutions with
built-in relevance, reliability, and responsibility.
Unit 2 Quiz — Get 2/2 correct to pass
Q: What is the primary reason the Orchestration Service uses a fixed sequence of modules?
✗ To make workflows rigid and prevent customization
✔ To ensure data is securely processed, grounded in facts, and compliant with enterprise standards
✗ To simplify LLM prompt wording for developers
✗ To minimize token usage by skipping unnecessary modules
Why: The order is fixed for security, data integrity, and compliance. Configuration WITHIN each module is flexible — the
fixed structure ensures reliability while flexibility ensures adaptability.
Q: What is the primary purpose of the Orchestration Service within the SAP Generative AI Hub?
✗ To manage SAP database connections for large datasets
✗ To coordinate and execute multiple user interface elements for AI-driven dashboards
✔ To coordinate and execute multiple AI models, services, and data flows within a unified workflow
✗ To train foundation models from scratch for custom enterprise needs
Why: Orchestration = systematic management and coordination of multiple AI models, services, and data flows to achieve a
unified business objective. Single API call triggers the entire multi-step pipeline.

ONE-PAGE QUICK CHEATSHEET — Module 3
Key Concept
What to Remember
AI Foundation
SAP's AI Operating System on BTP — backbone of SAP Business AI
4 GenAI Hub Interface Components
Model Library · Chat · Prompt Editor/Mgmt · Grounding Management
9-Step Orchestration Workflow
Input→Ground→Template→Mask→Filter→Translate→LLM→Filter→Translate
3 Model Hosting Types
Self-Hosted · SAP Managed (3rd-party LLMs) · SAP Hosted (SAP infrastructure)
AI Foundation — 4-Layer Architecture (Most Tested)
1. OS Interfaces
Joule Studio, AI Playground — user-facing dev/admin tools
2. AI Kernel
Manages agents, workloads, model lifecycle, resource scheduling
3. AI Integration
Data connectivity, model orchestration, workload management
4.

OS Interfaces
Joule Studio, AI Playground — user-facing dev/admin tools
2. AI Kernel
Manages agents, workloads, model lifecycle, resource scheduling
3. AI Integration
Data connectivity, model orchestration, workload management
4. Peripheral & Data
SAP + non-SAP data, IT infrastructure, partner models — raw compute
Orchestration Modules — Quick Recall
Grounding
Retrieves real business context → combats hallucinations
Templating
Dynamic placeholders filled at inference time → reusable prompts
Content Filtering
Blocks toxic/harmful content IN and OUT — configurable strictness
Data Masking
PII pseudonymized before LLM; can unmask in responses if needed
Translation
Input/output translation for multilingual enterprise use cases
4 Interaction Types — Quick Recall
Human → AI
User asks → AI responds (chatbot, Q&A;, summarization)
Software → AI
Backend feeds data → AI generates report/analysis
Human → Software via AI
Human describes intent → AI generates code/triggers action
Software → Software via AI
Automated trigger → AI → automated system action (no human loop)
Key Security & Compliance Facts
 SAP NEVER shares customer data with external vendors for model training
 Customer data is NEVER accessible to other customers in GenAI Hub
 SAP uses enterprise-grade LLMs that do NOT store API input or output
 Certifications: SOC 2, NIST, ISO — built-in governance by design
 Content Filtering can be set to 'RELAXED' mode to prevent blocking legitimate business terms
 Fixed workflow order = security/compliance; flexible configuration = business adaptability
SAP AI Core — Quick Facts
 Component of SAP BTP — hyperscaler-agnostic
 Resource Groups = virtual folders for ML resources within one account
 Default resource group auto-created; admins add/remove via SAP AI Launchpad
 Orchestration Service runs within SAP AI Core; requires Extended service plan
Good luck on your SAP Gen AI Certification! — Upload any remaining modules when ready.
